recommendation: Two method findings worth ratifying, both the sharpest evidence yet in this corpus: (1) the same 'suite cannot fail' defect class recurred SIX times in one mission, each new fix hiding a fresh instance one layer over from the last (suite-could-not-fail -> engine-suite-could-not-fail -> must-not-do-gate-could-not-fire -> CLI-guard-never-fired -> Examples-path-unbound -> fail-closed-was-a-proxy) -- and mutation sweeps found 13/16/28 defects per pass where reading found only 1-2, every one of the six dying to mutation and surviving every read. Recommend mutation-sweep-first become the default verification method for any mission whose subject is itself a measurement/verification instrument, reading treated as supplementary only. (2) 'Ablation-by-author has a blind spot' -- the author's own equivalence claim survived a 97-mutation self-authored sweep but was refuted in one shot by a cold judge (a comments-are-legal-anywhere gap the author's own rationale never considered); this is the sharpest instance yet of the already-known mutants-must-not-come-from-the-author rule. Separately, flag the mission's own recorded BLOCKING follow-up to the Council: the case-judge protocol has never been executed in production -- every judge.feature verdict is a static contract-binding read, because the impl-judge's own verification method invokes aced-case-judge (the subject itself), so it refused to grade it as circular. The mission proves the contract shape is right, not that the protocol works live.
evidence: 263-case-judge-protocol.plan.md: 'The same defect class recurred SIX times, each inside the fix for the last' -- enumerates all six named defects
same file: 'Reading has low yield; mutation has high yield ... mutation sweeps found 13, 16, and 28 [defects] ... every one of the six died to mutation and survived every read'
same file: 'Ablation-by-author has a blind spot ... a 97-mutation sweep agreed with and a cold judge refuted in one shot: comments are legal anywhere ... Mutants must not come from the author'
same file, recorded BLOCKING follow-up: 'Nobody ever executed the protocol ... the impl-judge's method is to invoke aced-case-judge -- the subject -- so it refused as circular'

recommendation: Two items. (1) A concrete, actionable HIGH-severity gate-mechanism gap distinct from the general pre-judge coverage-gap backlog: the edit-class classifier is blind to @rubric DocString content -- a frozen rubric's threshold can be silently gutted to 0 and the gate reports 'no-content-change', because step-text edits are caught while DocStrings (where every rubric actually lives) are not. This is a frozen-suite integrity hole, not a missing scenario, and deserves separate Council priority. (2) The mission re-derived and validated, at the cost of 5 judge rounds (a 4th-order recurrence of the already-known 'fix the rule at the site named without re-running it against the corpus it polices' failure), a design principle worth codifying: grade an untradeable behavior (the whole of what a persona produces) as one boolean @quality scenario per subject, never as a rubric dimension among several -- because a gate requiring every scenario to pass makes the boolean strictly stronger than a dimension that can be zeroed and bought back on unrelated points. Recommend this as a standing ACED spec-producer rule for any 'this output is the whole of what the persona produces' class of behavior.
evidence: 263-op6-m4-persona-voice-dimension.plan.md HIGH follow-up 1: 'the edit-class classifier is blind to @rubric DocString content. A frozen rubric can be gutted (threshold -> 0) and the gate reports no-content-change ... Step-text edits are caught; DocStrings are not -- where every rubric lives'

same file: 'Spec -- ALIGNED true (round 6). Rounds 1-5 ALIGNED false ... every round's root cause was the same: fixing the rule at the site the judge named without re-running it against the corpus it polices. It recurred to fourth order and cost five rounds'

same file: 'the boolean is strictly stronger than the dimension fix originally chosen ... the gate requires every scenario to pass, so voice is untradeable; a dimension inside a rubric can be zeroed and bought back on mechanics points'

recommendation: Two items, one a hazard to generalize and one a live risk to flag. (1) 'My own fix can create absorption' -- stating a new doctrine rule using the same worked example/apparatus a probe scenario already uses lifts the probe's illustration into the doctrine, and the probe then grades nothing. This mission ran exactly that check before landing anything: the proposed #254 fix was measured Δ=0 dead weight and NOT landed. Recommend any new doctrine rule authored to revive a dead rubric dimension be (a) stated abstractly with no worked example and (b) ablation-tested before landing, as a standing authoring rule. (2) Flag to the Council: this mission's own recorded BLOCKING follow-up directly undercuts the next mission's premise -- 9 of 11 @rubric scenarios in the repaired ssa-lowering suite remained unablated at handoff, so the claim that this repair left the suite 'a sound instrument for the capstone' was not established, yet op6-m6 (#224) proceeded and has since shipped against that same suite.
evidence: 263-op6-m5-ssa-lowering-repair.plan.md: '#254: REFUTED by measurement. The rule was NOT landed ... Δ = 0. The rule is dead weight. I ran the control and it survived -- the exact trap of ablating your own fix and reading survival as a pass'

same file, Hazards: 'My own fix can create absorption (#241's trap). Stating the direction rule with the probes' apparatus ... lifts the probe's illustration into the doctrine and the probe then grades nothing. State the rules abstractly; add no worked example'

263-op6-m5.7c4e91.jsonl (ledger followup, class: blocking): 'Contradicts this mission's own completion claim that the suite would be left a sound instrument for the capstone. Nine of eleven @rubric scenarios remain unablated and are unfalsified rather than earned ... The capstone's premise that this suite is sound is not established by this mission'

recommendation: Two items from op6-m6, the op6 capstone. (1) Enum-precision gap, with fresh concrete evidence: this mission's own combat log wrote three off-enum correction `cause` values ('ratified-rule-refuted-by-adversary', 'my-own-falsifier-planted-the-conclusion-twice', 'deleted-a-qualifier-my-model-made-unobservable') because none of the current 4-value enum (coverage-gap, design-overreach, spec-feature-contradiction, prose-impl-contradiction) fits a genuinely novel failure shape -- per governance an absent/off-enum cause fails closed, so the doctrine loop's own cause-matching is silently broken for this mission's richest corrections. Recommend the Council either grow the enum for a 'self-generated falsifier bakes in the premise under test' class, or explicitly ratify that free-text cause values are permitted for genuinely novel shapes pending growth. (2) The sharpest instance yet of a corpus-wide recurring lesson (cross-ref this shard's op6-m3 entry, 'mutants must not come from the author'): this mission's own measurement generator baked in the premise under test on two CONSECUTIVE rounds, each time producing a false zero-disagreement/redundant-machinery verdict that the owner ratified (adopting R4, then R8) and then had to refute and walk back. Recommend a standing rule: before presenting any zero-disagreement or dead-weight measurement as grounds for adopting or dropping a rule, verify the generator producing the comparison cases does not itself assume the property under test.
evidence: 224-barrier-fence-honoring.log.jsonl seq3 (correction, cause: ratified-rule-refuted-by-adversary): 'R4 was ratified on my argument ... The adversary refuted it: the mutex ... returns false when either side is empty ... My own harness had planted the conclusion by testing only project-covering touch-sets'

same file seq7 (correction, cause: my-own-falsifier-planted-the-conclusion-twice): 'The measurement I gave the owner as proof the offer clause was redundant was masked by my own generator ... This is the second consecutive round in which my harness baked in the assumption it was supposed to falsify'
same file seq8 (correction, cause: deleted-a-qualifier-my-model-made-unobservable): 'Unobservable in my model is not the same as unobservable in the subject'
cross-ref combat-log-governance: 'An absent or off-enum cause fails closed (it breaks cross-mission matchability)' -- three of this mission's four corrections used off-enum causes

recommendation: The op6-m5 follow-up chain (#308 ssa-lowering de-barrel, #370's spec-anchors de-barrel, #371 catches-misalignment Given re-cut) closed cleanly: each landed as a narrow Clearance-gated frozen-scenario edit, each ablation-validated before landing rather than argued, and each explicitly built on and superseded the prior CR's measurement instead of re-litigating it (#371's own commit message states it 'preserves the #254/#308 history it supersedes'). Recommend citing this chain as the reference precedent for handing off measurement state cleanly between a sequence of small Clearance-gated frozen-suite repairs. recommendation: Milestone retro -- op6 (master #263). Its release floor (m1 parse-guard, m2 discrimination bars, m3 case-judge protocol, m5 ssa-lowering repair, m6 barrier-fence capstone) is now fully merged; only m7 (a low-priority seat-and-retire bookkeeping node) and the master issue's own closure remain open. One meta-lesson threads literally every floor node and is the single strongest candidate to come out of this whole milestone: a self-authored measurement -- a fixture, a mutation set, an ablation generator, a falsifier -- is untrustworthy exactly when it is used to justify DROPPING or ADOPTING a rule, because the author's own blind spot gets baked into the instrument meant to catch it. m2 saw it (a pairwise consistency rule over-fired on the corpus's own general/specialization convention). m3 saw it twice (an author's own ablation missed a comment-is-legal-anywhere gap a cold judge caught in one shot; the same 'suite cannot fail' defect recurred through six relocations, found only by mutation, never by reading). m5 saw it (the proposed #254 fix's own zero-disagreement proof assumed the very invariant under test, caught before landing). m6/#224 saw it twice consecutively (R4 then R8 each ratified on the owner's argument, each refuted when the masked generator that 'proved' it was shown to assume its own conclusion). Recommend the Council ratify a standing SDD rule: any measurement offered as grounds for a rule-level decision (drop, adopt, threshold-set) must be produced or independently reviewed by a party other than the one proposing the rule, or ablated against a freshly, adversarially-constructed case -- never accepted on the proposer's own generator/harness alone.
evidence: cross-ref this shard seq1 (op6-m3: 'ablation-by-author has a blind spot', six-times-recurring unfalsifiable suite), seq3 (op6-m5: 'my own fix can create absorption', the #254 dead-weight catch), seq4 (op6-m6/#224: masked generator baking in its own premise on two consecutive rounds)

cross-ref strategy.f5152c seq1 (op6-m2, prior retro, unratified): 'a pairwise-consistency bar for discrimination ... over-fires on the corpus's own convention of a general scenario plus a narrower specialization sibling'

recommendation: One judge-iteration correction, closed by a genuine root-cause redesign rather than a patch: the spec-judge caught an uncovered CFG edge (a post-write verification-failed terminal) and a false 'every failure changed nothing' invariant; instead of adding a scenario to paper over the edge, the engine was redesigned to append the ignore rule as the last line so gitignore's last-match-wins semantics guarantee the ignore by construction, which removed the edge entirely rather than just covering it. Worth citing as a positive precedent: 'fix the invariant so the uncovered edge cannot occur' is a stronger move than 'add a scenario for the edge', when the design admits it. The correction's `cause` (post-write-verification-edge) is again off-enum but coverage-gap-shaped; folds into the same enum-precision reinforcement as this shard's other entries, not a new finding. Separately: this CR closes the M3 scope of the #304 umbrella only -- issue #304 (M1-M6) itself remains open, so this distillation targets the concluded M3 mission, not the umbrella.
evidence: 304-m3-eval-artifact-migration.log.jsonl: 'Cold spec-judge round 1 NOT-ALIGNED ... F1 an uncovered CFG edge (post-write verification-failed terminal X3) ... Root-cause fix: redesigned the engine to append the ignore rule as the LAST line so gitignore last-match-wins guarantees the ignore by construction; X3 removed ... Round 2 ALIGNED'
